mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, deteriorating labor market conditions signal severe economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of observations during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.5% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods following CPIAUCSL monthly increases exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion, which directly supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth periods show no statistically significant difference in 20-day forward returns compared to contraction periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines.
